Text: There’s another letter at the door.
…It’s been like this a lot lately.
I don’t know who’s sending them,
But they always end up here.
This paper… the edges look like they could be cut.
She left this behind and went out.
I thought it was just something she said in the heat of the moment.
So other people heard it too.
I still don’t dare open it.
That night… I didn’t even see her one last time.
She must still be blaming me, right?
What was she trying to say?
If I piece it together, would it form a complete thought?
But she… is never coming back.
There’s another letter at the door.
I thought there wouldn’t be any more after yesterday.
It’s as if something… still isn’t over.
I was at work almost every day.
I thought that if I just worked a little harder…
I didn’t realize it had become this much.
Those nights… I can barely remember them.
I just remember being exhausted all the time.
She used to stay with me back then.
And I never really paid attention.
… She wrote it over and over again.
Was she thinking about this the whole time?
You've done too much for me.
But Elena… this was always what I was supposed to do.
I never saw it as a burden.
I just wanted to make things easier for you.
You were the one who delivered the letter today as well.
I think… I’m starting to get used to your voice.
…What are you trying to make me understand today?
(a faint laugh) Her drawings are still as beautiful as ever.
She never told me about it.
She was enduring everything alone too.
So that’s what she was thinking back then?
I thought you'd be lighter without me.
You thought I’d feel lighter without you.
I just wanted to carry everything on my own.
But I never thought… I would lose you.
I’ve started waiting for you to appear.
It feels like… as long as you come,
I can remember a little more of her.
(a soft laugh) She always said she’d learn it someday.
Because I was always working late.
She wanted to help me… take a little of the load off.
A turn… no notice of a pedestrian.
But I can never convince myself of that.
The incident was just that—an unfortunate accident.
She was taken to the hospital alone.
And I… knew nothing at the time.
She didn’t leave because of me.
But why do I still feel…
like I failed to hold onto her?
The one who’s been bringing all these letters…
Now it’s my turn to tell you.
